description: To 'welotu' is to combine materials by moving them through one another until they are distributed for the intended preparation. The parts may remain visible, as beans and herbs do, or become hard to distinguish in dough. 'pharune' (include) places something within a set; mixing performs the physical combination.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move into true-mid front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let air pass along its sides, and hold stressed true-mid rounded 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'we' moves into the low lateral chamber of stressed 'lo'. Final 'tu' closes at the teeth and keeps the lips rounded. Two smooth openings meet one clean contact, a contour that can recall separate materials drawn into one motion.
usage_notes: The mixed materials form the object, often under plural marking. A vessel, tool, or intended texture may appear in its own phrase. Nominal use names mixing; the registered expression 'welotu keluo' names alloy as mixed metal.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Mixing may leave a marbled boundary visible or carry separate colours into one field. The desired surface decides when the hand stops.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Dough, plaster, dye, and clay each taught a different resistance through the same circling arm.

semantic_domains.activity: combining materials by moving them together
semantic_domains.physical: separate materials moving through one another
